name: ◁ Bulgur Salad
servings: prep time: 10 minutes, plus 2 hours chilling

ingredients.item: 1 cup (180 g) coarse bulgur (see Note), rinsed and drained
ingredients.original: 1 cup (180 g) coarse bulgur (see Note), rinsed and drained

ingredients.item: 1 large red bell pepper, small diced
ingredients.original: 1 large red bell pepper, small diced

ingredients.item: ½ cup (25 g) finely chopped fresh parsley
ingredients.original: ½ cup (25 g) finely chopped fresh parsley

ingredients.item: ¼ cup (30 g) slivered almonds (or substitute with other nuts or seeds)
ingredients.original: ¼ cup (30 g) slivered almonds (or substitute with other nuts or seeds)

ingredients.item:
DRESSING
¼ cup (60 ml) fresh lemon juice
2 tablespoons pomegranate molasses
ingredients.original:
DRESSING
¼ cup (60 ml) fresh lemon juice
2 tablespoons pomegranate molasses

ingredients.item: 1 TO MAKE THE BULGUR: In a medium saucepan, heat the oil over medium heat. Add the onion and cook, stirring often, until softened, 5 to 6 minutes.
ingredients.original: 1 TO MAKE THE BULGUR: In a medium saucepan, heat the oil over medium heat. Add the onion and cook, stirring often, until softened, 5 to 6 minutes.

ingredients.item: 2 Add the salt, bulgur, and 1½ cups (360 ml) of water. Bring to a boil, then cover and let simmer over low heat for 20 minutes. Fluff it with a fork and check doneness; the grains should be soft but not mushy. If the grains are not soft, add 2 tablespoons of water, cover again, and let stand for 10 more minutes with the heat off.
ingredients.original: 2 Add the salt, bulgur, and 1½ cups (360 ml) of water. Bring to a boil, then cover and let simmer over low heat for 20 minutes. Fluff it with a fork and check doneness; the grains should be soft but not mushy. If the grains are not soft, add 2 tablespoons of water, cover again, and let stand for 10 more minutes with the heat off.

ingredients.item: 3 TO MAKE THE SALAD: Transfer the cooked bulgur to a medium bowl. Add the cucumbers, tomato, red pepper, parsley, green onions, and raisins.
ingredients.original: 3 TO MAKE THE SALAD: Transfer the cooked bulgur to a medium bowl. Add the cucumbers, tomato, red pepper, parsley, green onions, and raisins.

ingredients.item: 4 TO MAKE THE DRESSING: Add the lemon juice, pomegranate molasses, oil, salt, and pepper to a small jar or bowl. Whisk or seal the jar and shake the ingredients to incorporate well.
ingredients.original: 4 TO MAKE THE DRESSING: Add the lemon juice, pomegranate molasses, oil, salt, and pepper to a small jar or bowl. Whisk or seal the jar and shake the ingredients to incorporate well.

ingredients.item: 5 Pour the dressing over the salad and toss to combine well. Refrigerate for at least 2 hours.
ingredients.original: 5 Pour the dressing over the salad and toss to combine well. Refrigerate for at least 2 hours.

ingredients.item: 6 Toast the slivered almonds in a small, dry skillet over medium heat for 3 to 4 minutes, until lightly golden, stirring continuously. Add them to the salad right before serving.
ingredients.original: 6 Toast the slivered almonds in a small, dry skillet over medium heat for 3 to 4 minutes, until lightly golden, stirring continuously. Add them to the salad right before serving.

ingredients.item:
Note
If you can’t find coarse bulgur, you can use fine bulgur, but it requires no cooking. Simply soak 1 cup (180 g) of fine bulgur in 2 cups (480 ml) of boiling water for 10 to 12 minutes. Season with ½ teaspoon kosher salt, drizzle with 2 tablespoons of olive oil, and then fluff it with a fork.
ingredients.original:
Note
If you can’t find coarse bulgur, you can use fine bulgur, but it requires no cooking. Simply soak 1 cup (180 g) of fine bulgur in 2 cups (480 ml) of boiling water for 10 to 12 minutes. Season with ½ teaspoon kosher salt, drizzle with 2 tablespoons of olive oil, and then fluff it with a fork.
